# ghflow_pull_request (Resource)

Opens a pull request in a GitHub repository. `title`, `body`, and `base_ref` can be updated in place; changing `owner`, `repository`, or `head_ref` replaces the PR.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `base_ref` (String) The branch you want the changes pulled into, e.g. `main`.
- `head_ref` (String) The branch containing the changes to merge. For a cross-repo PR use `owner:branch`.
- `owner` (String) Owner of the repository (user or organization).
- `repository` (String) Name of the repository.
- `title` (String) Title of the pull request.

### Optional

- `body` (String) Body (description) of the pull request.
- `close_on_destroy` (Boolean) If true (default), `destroy` closes the pull request. If false, `destroy` only removes the resource from state and leaves the PR open.

### Read-Only

- `head_sha` (String) SHA of the head commit of the pull request.
- `html_url` (String) Web URL of the pull request.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, set to the pull request number.
- `merged` (Boolean) Whether the pull request has been merged.
- `number` (Number) The pull request number.
- `state` (String) State of the pull request: `open` or `closed`.
